analysis.consistency: Consistent. The spell details (level, casting time, range, components, duration, classes) align with typical D&D 5e spell structures.
analysis.quality: High. The description is detailed, clearly outlining the scope (1 mile square, terrain alteration, structure addition/alteration) and limitations (no creatures, illusory nature of removed objects).

mechanics.casting_time: 10 minutes (Long duration for casting suggests a high-level, preparatory effect)
mechanics.range: Sight (Appropriate for an area-of-effect visual/sensory illusion)
mechanics.components: V, S (Standard for complex mental/sensory effects)
mechanics.duration: 10 days (Long duration supports its use as a sustained magical feature for area control or deception)
mechanics.area_of_effect: Up to 1 mile square (Very large scale)
mechanics.interaction: It alters terrain to affect movement (difficult/impassable terrain), meaning it has tangible mechanical effects on navigation, even though it's an illusion. Objects removed disappear, reinforcing its illusory nature.
mechanics.countermeasures: Truesight sees the true form, but tactile/olfactory elements persist, making it a complex illusion that even True Sight users must contend with physically interacting against.
